text: Worked on the living-workspace "working" instance, starting with an audit of how views are generated and rendered from templates and how cleanly the data-reading functions are abstracted from the rendering layer. Reframed the system to anchor the experience on the server and rewrote the README so it opens with a clear explanation of what the system is plus a getting-started guide. Made the workflow explicit by adding an evaluation step to each pass (optimistically assumed to pass, with rework only on failure) and debugged the dashboard for the "Fascia Emotion" item, where the current phase, next action, and awaiting-title-abstract counts were rendering blank/zero. Clarified confusing workflow stages, notably the "title abstract" step being used as an evaluation-decision point. Ended in a naming discussion: researching how this repo is not quite a Claude Code plugin but an "agent-native repo," and trying to converge on / coin a proper term and name for that category.
